======= RMM04856Francis G. Scheider papers4856Compiled by M.E. WarrenDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Feb. 20022B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
ornitThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:54:06 UTC.ENG12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Francis G. Scheider papers
Scheider, Francis G., 1932-1989.
4856
9.3 cubic feet. 9.3 cubic feet.
1940-1988. Subject files, field records, reports, checklists, photographs, and other papers. Subject files, field records, reports, and checklists, photographs, and other papers of an expert birder from the Rochester, New York area; records pertain mostly to Central New York State, but include accounts of trips to Alaska, Florida, and other places. Collection material in English 2315931
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
Mar. 1994
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, Feb. 2002
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Francis G. Scheider was a birder, amateur ornithologist, and field collaborator of the Cornell University Laboratory of Ornithology.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Subject files, field records, reports, and checklists, photographs, and other papers of an expert birder from the Rochester, New York area; records pertain mostly to Central New York State, but include accounts of trips to Alaska, Florida, and other places.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Alphabetical subject files
Boxes 1-3, 10
Series II. Chronological records and reports
Boxes 3-12
Cite As:
Francis G. Scheider Papers, #4856. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Ornithology. Bird watchers -- New York (State) Birds -- Societies, etc. Cornell University. Laboratory of Ornithology
2315931 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I. Alphabetical subject files
Scope and Contents
Includes birding trips to Alaska and other states; New York State Breeding Bird Atlas Project; Kingbird; Christmas counts; New York State Avian Record Committee; correspondence with individuals.Alaska Trip197111-2Alaska II Trip197913-4Alaska II - Wings Inc.
